description: Baseline eval prompts. should_trigger entries must invoke the skill; should_not_trigger entries must not. Compatible with skill-creator's run_loop.py.

should_trigger.prompt: Walk me through the accountable category for a library self-checkout kiosk — does the schema capture multi-party responsibility?
Two elements in ai__storage feel redundant to me — can you audit the category and tell me if they overlap?
Is ai__risks_mitigation complete for a generative AI system, or are there risk classes we don't cover yet?
Audit the ai__decision category for us — I want a coverage map, any overlaps, and anything we're missing.
What's ai__rights missing for modern appeals processes? The elements feel dated when I try to map a 2026 recourse flow onto them.
Does DTPR cover third-party training data provenance inside the ai__data category, or is there a gap we should close?

should_not_trigger.prompt: Refactor this Python function to use a dataclass instead of a dict.
What's the difference between a Docker image and a container?
Describe our facial-recognition parking kiosk at the downtown garage as a DTPR datachain.
Draft a new element for LLM hallucination disclosure with a title, description, and symbol direction — I want to add it to the schema.
Should the ai datachain-type retire the ai__storage category entirely and fold it into ai__retention? Critique the overall shape.
Grade the element accept_deny against the comprehension rubric — I just want a readability check, no schema changes.
Translate the ai__risks_mitigation category name and description into Khmer for our pilot in Phnom Penh.
Generate an SVG icon for a new element I'm thinking about adding to the risks category.
